chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Manufacturing Operations. (3-0). Credit 3. Analytical principles of manufacturing systems design, analysis and control; emphasis placed on stochastic analysis; role of variability and impact on cycle time; push versus pull production strategies including Kanban and constant WIP control; probability, queuing theory, Little's Law, heavy traffic approximations, and queuing networks. Prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ISEN 340 and MATH 304; junior or senior classification.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content:
Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in ISEN 340 and MATH 304;
junior or senior classification.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Homework: 0%
Exam 1: 1/3% (Thursday of Week 4, February 5)
Exam 2: 1/3% (Thursday of Week 8, March 5) The week before Spring Break.
Exam 3: 1/3% (Tuesday, April. 9)
Exam 4: 1/3% (the final period is scheduled for 1-3 pm, Tuesday, May 5)
Note: Best three of the four exams will be used.
Grades assigned are A for 90%-100%, B for 80%-89.9%, C for 70%–79.9%, D for 60%-69.9% and F for less than 60%.
Due to no grading support for this class, I will not pickup and grade homework assignments. Homework is a major learning experience and is meant to help prepare you for the exams. As such, I do not expect homework to be done independently. A suggestion for the homework process is to do each problem on your own, then check with other students as to the correctness of your solution, and when incorrect, use that information as an opportunity to improve your knowledge in this course. We will cover questions and short discussions of the homework solutions at the start of each lecture period. If something is not understood that a good time to visit my office or to setup a Zoom help session for clarifications or ask about it during our weekly Zoom help sessions.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
| Week | Topic | Required Reading |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Introduction to Factory Models: Definitions, Notation, modeling, performance, deterministic vs. stochastic | Chapter 2 |
| 2 | Single Workstation Factory Models: Queueing models | Chapter 3 |
| 3 | Single Workstation Factory Models: Markov modes | Chapter 3 |
| 4 | Single Workstation Factory Models: Balance Equations, Exam 1 | Chapter 3 |
| 5 | Single Workstation Factory Models: M/M/1 models, G/G/1 approximations | Chapter 3 |
| 6 | Processing Time Variability: Breakdown and repairs, other variability | Chapter 4 |
| 7 | Single Product Factory Models: Departure process | Chapter 5 |
| 8 | Single Product Factory Models: serial systems, Exam 2 | Chapter 5 |
| 9 | Single Product Factory Models: queueing networks | Chapter 5 |
| 10 | Multiple Product Factory Models: Multiple Product Flow, Service time variability, process step modeling, group technology | Chapter 6 |
| 11 | Models of Various Forms of Batching Batch moves, set up reduction, batch service | Chapter 7 |
| 12 | WIP Limiting Control Strategies: Closed Queueing Networks, General | Chapter 8 |
| 13 | service times, Exam 3 WIP Limiting Control Strategies: multiple products, CONWIP, scheduling rules | Chapter 8 |
| 14 | Kanban Models, Exam 4 | Notes and Chapter 9 |
